statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input contains two lines. The first line contains a single integer, m (1 <= m <= 10^5), and the second line contains m space-separated integers in the range [1, 10^5] representing the pages of a book.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a single integer representing the maximum number of non-stapled pages.
statement.notes: A page is stapled if it is in a group with at least 2 pages. Two pages are considered to be in the same group if their difference is less than or equal to 1.

examples.explanation: The 1st page is stapled to the 2nd page. The 3rd page is stapled to the 4th page.

hints.text: A simple greedy approach can be used here.

editorial.title: Greedy Algorithm
editorial.intuition: We can sort the pages. Then we can try to put each pair of adjacent numbers together.
editorial.bruteForce: Try all possible orderings of the numbers.
editorial.optimalSolution: Sort the pages and try to put each pair of adjacent numbers together.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the greedy algorithm can be proved by induction.
